title: Fixing Broken HTML Anchor Tags with Missing 'href' Attributes

statement.markdown: Write a function that validates if all anchor tags in a given HTML string have a valid 'href' attribute. An anchor tag is considered valid if it has a 'href' attribute and its value is a valid URL. A valid URL is a string that starts with either 'http://' or 'https://'.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string containing HTML code, e.g. 'Example Broken Link'
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether all anchor tags have a valid 'href' attribute
statement.notes: The function should return True if all anchor tags have a valid 'href' attribute, False otherwise.

constraints.description: The length of the input HTML string

examples.explanation: The second anchor tag is missing a 'href' attribute
The second anchor tag has an empty 'href' attribute
All anchor tags have a valid 'href' attribute

hints.text: First hint text: Check if the 'href' attribute exists in each anchor tag
